hero.title: Good coffee, no fuss
hero.subtitle: We roast small batches every week and brew them the way each bean deserves. Come for the espresso, stay for the quiet corner by the window.
hero.imageAlt: Barista pouring latte art at the counter

items.title: Espresso bar
items.description: Single-origin espresso, flat whites, and filter brews. Ask what's on the grinder this week — it changes.

items.title: Weekend brunch
items.description: Saturdays and Sundays until 14:00: eggs, sourdough, and a short seasonal menu. No reservations, it moves fast.

items.title: Cakes & pastries
items.description: Baked each morning by a small local bakery we've worked with since day one.

items.title: Beans to take home
items.description: 250g bags of our weekly roast, whole bean or ground for your brewer. We'll write brewing notes on the bag.

about.title: A small roastery with tables
about.subtitle: Zrno means 'bean' — that's roughly the whole story.
about.body: We started roasting at home in 2019, sold bags to friends, and eventually needed a bigger machine and a place to put it. The café grew around the roaster, not the other way round. We keep the menu short so everything on it is something we're proud of, and we'd rather tell you which brew suits your taste than upsell you the most expensive one.

items.title: Fast wifi
items.description: Fiber connection and plenty of sockets. Laptops welcome on weekdays.

items.title: Dog friendly
items.description: Water bowls at the door and treats behind the counter.

items.title: Vinyl evenings
items.description: First Friday of the month someone brings records. Come early.

items.title: Quiet mornings
items.description: Before 9:00 it's just you, the grinder, and the newspaper.

items.title: Oat & almond milk
items.description: No surcharge. Bad coffee is the only thing we charge extra for — by not serving it.

items.title: Loyalty card
items.description: Tenth coffee free. Paper card, no app, no tracking.

items.alt: Coffee roaster in the back room
items.caption: The roaster

items.alt: Brunch plates on a wooden table
items.caption: Weekend brunch

items.alt: Shelf with coffee bags for sale
items.caption: Beans to go

items.alt: Guests and a dog by the entrance
items.caption: Regulars

items.name: Marko V.
items.text: Best filter coffee in town and nobody gives you a look for staying three hours with a laptop.

items.name: Nina P.
items.text: The brunch is small but everything on the plate is right. Get there before 11 or be ready to wait.

items.name: Luka T.
items.text: I buy their beans every other week. The brewing notes on the bag actually make a difference.

items.question: Do you take reservations?
items.answer: No — it's first come, first served. Mornings and late afternoons are usually easy to find a seat.

items.question: Can I work from the café?
items.answer: On weekdays, absolutely — there's fast wifi and sockets by most tables. On weekend brunch hours we ask laptops to take a break.

items.question: Do you sell beans?
items.answer: Yes, 250g bags of whatever we roasted that week, whole bean or ground to order. Ask for brewing notes.

items.question: Is there anything decaf or non-coffee?
items.answer: A proper decaf espresso, a small tea selection, house lemonade, and hot chocolate in winter.

cta.title: Come by for a cup
cta.subtitle: Near the city park, open from 7:30 on weekdays.

contact.title: Find us
contact.subtitle: Two minutes from the park entrance.

footer.description: Specialty coffee bar and small roastery in Novi Sad.
